title: The roar from Zion
summary_text: The LORD roars from Zion and judges Damascus, Gaza, Tyre, Edom, Ammon, and Moab for cruelty. Each oracle strips away the idea that pagan violence is hidden from God. His voice over the nations frames the book's warning to Israel.
key_points: The LORD roars from Zion and judges Damascus, Gaza, Tyre, Edom, Ammon, and Moab for cruelty.
Each oracle strips away the idea that pagan violence is hidden from God.
His voice over the nations frames the book's warning to Israel.

title: Judgment on Judah and Israel
summary_text: Judah and Israel are judged for rejecting the LORD's law and oppressing the poor. Their privilege heightens their guilt because they knew better and still turned aside. Election becomes a burden of responsibility.
key_points: Judah and Israel are judged for rejecting the LORD's law and oppressing the poor.

title: Israel under scrutiny
summary_text: Israel is the only family the LORD has known, so its sin receives special scrutiny. Disaster will come, yet God still speaks through prophets before the blow falls. The chapter ties revelation to accountability.
key_points: Israel is the only family the LORD has known, so its sin receives special scrutiny.
Disaster will come, yet God still speaks through prophets before the blow falls.

title: Prepare to meet your God
summary_text: The wealthy women of Samaria are condemned for crushing the poor while demanding comfort. God had already disciplined them by drought, blight, and plague, yet they would not return. Israel is told to prepare to meet its God.
key_points: The wealthy women of Samaria are condemned for crushing the poor while demanding comfort.

title: Seek the LORD and live
summary_text: The prophet sings a lament over fallen Israel and calls the people to seek the LORD and live. Sacrifices are worthless without justice, and judgment will overtake those who hate reproof. The day of the LORD is darkness for empty religion.
key_points: The prophet sings a lament over fallen Israel and calls the people to seek the LORD and live.

title: Woe to the complacent
summary_text: The elite of Zion and Samaria trust in luxury and military strength. Their feasting will end in exile, and their first place among the nations will become shame. Secure living detached from compassion is condemned.
key_points: The elite of Zion and Samaria trust in luxury and military strength.
Their feasting will end in exile, and their first place among the nations will become shame.
Secure living detached from compassion is condemned.

title: Locusts, fire, and the plumb line
summary_text: Two visions of judgment show locusts and fire, but Amos intercedes and God relents. A plumb line then reveals that Israel is out of true and will not be spared. Amaziah opposes the prophet and receives a hard word of exile.
key_points: Two visions of judgment show locusts and fire, but Amos intercedes and God relents.

title: A basket of summer fruit
summary_text: A basket of summer fruit signals that the end has come for Israel's ripe corruption. Greedy merchants cheat with false weights and cannot wait for the Sabbath and new moon to pass. God will send a famine of hearing his words.

title: The fallen altar and final hope
summary_text: The altar is struck and no sinner can hide from the falling house. Yet the book ends with restoration: David's booth will be raised, the nations will be gathered, and Israel will be planted again. Judgment is real, but mercy has the last word.
key_points: The altar is struck and no sinner can hide from the falling house.
Yet the book ends with restoration: David's booth will be raised, the nations will be gathered, and Israel will be planted again.
Judgment is real, but mercy has the last word.
